Background

Lack of stromal cell detection (lack of nuclei)

Cell in close proximity

Cohort used

10 slides

Proposed approach

Divide each TIFF image input (33 channels) into small tiles (tested so 32x32x33, will test 128x128x33) 
Train a CNN VAE with those images (basic architecture) 
Extract latent space for each tile, using trained model 
Visualize latent space using T-SNE and UMAP 
Association of latent space (clustering, classifier?) with molecular/clinical traits of the cohort.

Proposed approach

Divide each TIFF image input (33 channels) into small tiles (tested so 32x32x33, will test 128x128x33) 
Train a CNN VAE with those images (basic architecture) 
Extract latent space for each tile, using trained model 
Visualize latent space using T-SNE and UMAP 
Association of latent space (clustering, classifier?) with molecular/clinical traits of the cohort.

CNN architecture - VAE

32 dim

CNN architecture - VAE

32 dim

Computing time and resources

28 cores, 224 gb of ram, 2 GPU’s Tesla P100 16gb VRAM
Processing time: with one slide, 4 days for running basic model for 120 epochs
Input: 151 cores, ~8000 patches (32x32x33) per core

Questions

Feedback on overall approach
Divide channels into separate images? (for each position, 4 patches of 32x32x8 instead of one 32x32x32?)
Decisions on model validation - VAE
Train-validation set for validation loss measurement?
How to compute latent space for validation set? Retrain on full data?

VQ-VAE
Vanilla AE
VAE
Others
Contrastive learning instead of VAE
Adapt Histoencoder to CyCIF images? Can we use computation resource of LUMI
Adapt HistoPrep to CyCIF images?
Latent space visualization, T-SNE, UMAP?
